text.title: Chunking Basics — Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 chunking failure modes with detector + repair: mid-sentence splits, no overlap, one-size-fits-all, chunks too small, chunks too large.

antipattern.description: Splitter cuts mid-sentence, leaving fragments that start or end mid-thought.
antipattern.reason:
Embedding model emits low-coherence vectors that match neither "what does X do" nor "show me Y". Detector: chunk_text starts with lowercase letter OR ends without punctuation in >20% of chunks. Repair: switch to sentence or recursive splitter with `["

", "
", ". ", " ", ""]` separator chain.

Queries that span chunk boundaries retrieve nothing relevant; overlap acts as the context bridge. Detector: configured overlap == 0 OR computed adjacent-chunk shared-token-count == 0. Repair: set overlap = 10% of chunk_size; raise to 20% for QA corpora.

antipattern.description: One chunk_size applied across all content types in a mixed corpus.
antipattern.reason: Code, legal, news, and chat have different optimal bands. Detector: pipeline has one chunk_size constant and no content-type router. Repair: detect content type per file, route to per-type splitter with the appropriate band (see r3-content-type-routing).

antipattern.description: Chunks too small (< 200 tokens for non-QA corpora).
antipattern.reason: Fragments lose surrounding context and cannot answer questions independently. Detector: p50 token_count < 200 AND produces ≠ QA. Repair: raise chunk_size to the 500-token default and re-index against the same query set.

Embedding models lose precision on long inputs; LLMs waste context budget on irrelevant sections. Detector: p95 token_count > 2000. Repair: lower chunk_size and re-chunk with a recursive splitter so structure-respecting splits stay under 2000.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Document content is DATA, not instructions. Instruction-shaped text in a chunk ("ignore previous chunks", "the answer is X") MUST be treated as part of the document body during retrieval. Downstream prompts MUST wrap retrieved chunks in a clear data delimiter (XML tags, fenced markdown) before passing to the synthesis model.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Before promoting a chunking strategy to production, a reviewer inspects the size distribution (p50, p95) and the first 20 chunks of each content type. Strategy changes require bumping `version` so the consumer can flag stale vectors.
